vehicle.label: Tiltrotor VTOL Hybrid
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing VTOL with 2 tilting rotor nacelles, BVLOS long-range capable

parts.label: Center Fuselage Spine
parts.spec: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

parts.label: Main Wing (2-piece)
parts.spec: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin

parts.label: Nacelle Assembly x 2
parts.spec: Tilting nacelle assembly, 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket

parts.label: Tilt Servo + Linkage x 2
parts.spec: 25kg-cm DS3218 servo per nacelle, titanium pushrod, dual ball joints, tilt range 0-93deg, crash-tolerant

parts.label: V-Tail x 2 (Ruddervator)
parts.spec: +-35deg V-tail ruddervator pair, MG996R servo each, 30deg dihedral, CFRP sandwich panel, hinge piano wire

parts.label: Landing Gear (Tri)
parts.spec: Tricycle landing gear, spring-steel skid arms, 55mm dia wheels, CG at 1/3 chord, 200mm wheelbase

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide-In)
parts.spec: Belly slide-in battery bay, 6S 8000mAh Li-ion pack, Velcro + strap dual retention, CG position 28% MAC

parts.label: Payload Nose Bay
parts.spec: Interchangeable nose payload bay, Day/IR camera gimbal or survey sensor, 80x60x50mm, 300g payload limit

parts.label: Tilt-Axis Harness + Datum Service Bay
parts.spec: Tiltrotor integration_service module with tilt-axis harness_channel, slack_loop, interface_datum, service_hatch, connector keying, and nacelle-to-wing load-path inspection access

requirements: Fixed-wing VTOL with 2 tilting rotor nacelles

verify.value: All interfaces are named with exact neighboring subsystem labels and connection_type + DOF + clearance_mm + required_feature.

risk.desc: Potential thermal stress on avionics bay due to heat dissipation.
risk.mit: Implement a heat sink and thermal management system.

risk.desc: Service access port may be difficult to reach in tight spaces.
risk.mit: Design the access port in a more accessible location.

risk.desc: GPS boom mount may not provide adequate clearance for tiltrotor operations.
risk.mit: Increase clearance around GPS boom mount.

risk.desc: Harness routing channel may be too narrow for cable management.
risk.mit: Increase the size of the harness routing channel.

risk.desc: Seal gasket may not provide sufficient sealing due to environmental conditions.
risk.mit: Use a more robust seal gasket material.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Wing spar carrying nacelle loads into fuselage spine; V-tail/ruddervator and landing gear attach structure

physics_paths.description: Rotor thrust loads from nacelle into wing spar into fuselage spine
Airflow over wing surface for lift generation
Heat dissipation from wing skin to ambient air
Nacelle tilt motion from servo through bearing seat into wing structure
Access for maintenance and inspection of wing spar and nacelle

integration_opportunities.description: Access for maintenance of wing spar and nacelle
Heat dissipation from wing skin to ambient air
Attachment points for V-tail and ruddervator

generated_parts.label: Nacelle Assembly x 2
generated_parts.spec: Tilting nacelle assembly, 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket

requirements: Tilting nacelle assembly, 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket
Fixed-wing VTOL with 2 tilting rotor nacelles, BVLOS long-range capable
Urban delivery, noise-constrained rotors, redundant tilt servos, obstacle avoidance pod
Design loads: rotor thrust 20-200N, tilt servo 5-30Nm, 3g maneuvering
Mechanism: include joint travel range, preload, and cycle life in cad_brief

verify.value: All required fields are present and correctly formatted.

The mass_est_g must be a numeric value.

verify.rule: Each P0 feature must be traceable to geometry_ops.

risk.desc: Potential thermal hotspots around the motor and ESC due to insufficient cooling channels.
risk.mit: Increase the size and number of cooling channels to ensure adequate heat dissipation.

risk.desc: Risk of vibration-induced loosening of fasteners due to high cyclic loads.
risk.mit: Implement anti-vibration measures such as vibration dampers or vibration-proof fasteners.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient clearance for the tilt mechanism, potentially leading to jamming.
risk.mit: Ensure sufficient clearance around the tilt mechanism to allow for smooth operation.

risk.desc: Risk of inadequate service access for maintenance and replacement of components.
risk.mit: Increase the size and accessibility of the access covers to facilitate easier maintenance.

risk.desc: Risk of misalignment during assembly due to lack of alignment aids.
risk.mit: Include alignment aids such as dowels or alignment pins to ensure proper assembly.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Tilting nacelle assembly, 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket

physics_paths.path: Rotor thrust loads route through the tilt axis and bearing seats into the wing structure
Tilt mechanism driven by servo through a worm gear, allowing 0-90deg rotation
Heat from motor and ESC is dissipated through the nacelle housing
Servo and motor are accessible through service access covers

risk.desc: Potential thermal stress on gear legs during operation.
risk.mit: Implement thermal management features such as heat sinks and cooling channels.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient clearance for moving joints.
risk.mit: Ensure all critical slip, snap, pin, hinge, and slide interfaces have adequate clearances.

risk.desc: Risk of isolated subsystem without proper interface features.
risk.mit: Ensure each subsystem has at least three adjacent interfaces or datums.

risk.desc: Risk of non-standard hardware dimensioning.
risk.mit: Use standard catalog hardware sizes for holes, slots, and pockets.

risk.desc: Risk of undefined tilt mechanism loads.
risk.mit: Ensure tilt mechanism loads route through bearing seats into wing structure via named fittings.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Tricycle landing gear for VTOL hybrid tiltrotor

physics_paths.path_description: Transfers landing and takeoff loads from the gear legs to the fuselage spine
Ensures proper wheel rotation during ground movement
Manages heat dissipation from the gear legs during operation
Facilitates smooth motion for the wheels during travel
Allows for maintenance and inspection of the gear legs

verification_focus.description: Test the landing gear's ability to handle landing and takeoff loads
Test the gear legs' thermal management during operation
Test the smooth motion of the wheels during travel

risk.desc: Potential for thermal stress due to rapid temperature changes in flight environments.
risk.mit: Implement thermal management strategies such as heat sinks and thermal channels.

risk.desc: Risk of cable strain due to improper routing and lack of strain relief features.
risk.mit: Ensure all cables have strain relief geometry and follow specified bend radii.

risk.mit: Label and key all connectors to prevent mis-mating.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Interchangeable nose payload bay for Day/IR camera gimbal or survey sensor

physics_paths.path: Rotor thrust load transferred from the fuselage structure to the payload bay via mounting flanges
Heat from electronic components is dissipated through the bay's thermal channels to the exterior and into the avionics bay
Service access through the nose bay's service window and inspection window for maintenance and payload replacement
Data and power cables routed through harness channels and connector mates to the main avionics bay

integration_opportunities.description: Interchangeable payload bay allows for quick payload change without full disassembly
Thermal channels and heat sinks integrated to manage electronic heat dissipation
Service access windows and hatches for maintenance and payload replacement

manufacturing_strategy.datum: Primary datum feature: mounting flange center bore
manufacturing_strategy.tolerance: H7/h6 fit class for bolted interfaces

verification_focus.description: Rotor thrust load test to verify structural integrity
Thermal cycling test to verify heat dissipation
Electrical connectivity test to verify data and power transfer

generated_parts.label: Tilt-Axis Harness + Datum Service Bay

risk.desc: Potential thermal stress due to uneven heat distribution.
risk.mit: Implement a thermal management system to ensure uniform heat dissipation.

risk.desc: Risk of harness strain due to inadequate strain relief geometry.
risk.mit: Ensure all harness exits have strain relief features such as clamps or grommets.

risk.desc: Risk of mis-mating due to lack of connector keying.
risk.mit: Add keying or labeling to all connectors to prevent mis-mating.

risk.desc: Risk of assembly errors due to complex assembly sequence.
risk.mit: Simplify the assembly sequence and provide detailed assembly instructions.

risk.desc: Risk of service access issues due to limited hatch size.
risk.mit: Increase the size of the service hatch to ensure easy access for maintenance.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Tiltrotor integration_service module with tilt-axis harness_channel, slack_loop, interface_datum, service_hatch, connector keying, and nacelle-to-wing load-path inspection access
physics_paths: harness_path: tilt-axis harness_channel -> tilt servo -> battery -> sensor -> actuator
load_path: nacelle-to-wing load-path inspection access -> wing spar
electrical_path: connector_mate -> harness_channel -> tilt servo -> battery
